terms.definition: Positive scalar η that scales each gradient-based parameter update; also called step size in optimization.
Synonym for learning rate: the magnitude of the parameter change per optimization step along −∇L.
Rule that sets η as a function of training step or epoch (constant, step decay, exponential decay, warmup, etc.).
Initial or reference η₀ from which scheduled or adaptive methods scale per-step updates.
Common code name for learning rate in frameworks (e.g. optimizer argument `lr=1e-3`).
